decision_question: Should we run a seven-day validation for an AI study product before building a broader MVP?
context_summary: The room narrowed the idea to one repeated study bottleneck and recommended validating it with a small cohort before building a full product.
key_assumptions: Students have a repeated study moment that happens often enough to test retention.
A semi-manual workflow can produce useful feedback before a full app exists.

known_evidence.text: The room summary selected a narrow study bottleneck rather than a broad AI tutor.
The first student segment and acquisition channel are still unknown.
Interview five students, pick one bottleneck, and define retention thresholds before building.

open_questions: Which student segment should be tested first?
Which acquisition channel can produce the first cohort?

risk_flags.why_it_matters: The first test must separate curiosity from repeated use.
A full platform would make early retention signals harder to interpret.

recommended_panel.responsibility: Compress the product into one sharp repeated experience.
Check validation discipline, downside, and kill rules.
Check whether the thinnest technical loop can support real use.

notes: This fixture validates handoff schema shape only.
It is not host-live validation and not provider-live validation.
